[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2017-11-02
[TITLE]: Belgian Killed in Terror Attack Was Mom to Two Young Boys; Husband Says 'Loss Is Unbearable'
[METADATA/INTRO]: A Belgian woman killed in a terrorist attack in Manhattan this week was an "amazing wife" and "the most wonderful mom to our two little boys," her grieving husband said. Ann-Laure Decadt, 31, from Staden, West Flanders, Belgium, was one...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A Belgian woman killed in a terrorist attack in Manhattan this week was an "amazing wife" and "the most wonderful mom to our two little boys," her grieving husband said. 
 Ann-Laure Decadt, 31, from Staden, West Flanders, Belgium, was one of eight victims killed when the attacker drove a truck for blocks through a bike lane on Tuesday before crashing into a school bus. 
 Her husband, Alexander Naessens, told Dutch language newspaper Het Laatste Nieuws that Decadt was vacationing in New York with her two sisters and mother and was biking along the West Side Highway when the attack happened.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2014
[TITLE]: Imprisoned by alcohol: Life on the outside
[METADATA/INTRO]: Imprisoned by alcohol: Life on the outside BOURNE — Desiree Azarian walks out of the women's cell pod at the Barnstable County Correctional Facility on a crisp, bright April morning....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: By September, she was spending nights with a longtime former boyfriend, also with a severe alcohol addiction, in a West Yarmouth motel room. 
 Later that week, Desiree said she didn't want to go into a sober home or a halfway house. She wanted to stay at the hotel and help her friend, who she feared would soon die. 
 "I'm not really sober enough to be alone," she admitted. 
 She could also see where she was heading. 
 "With my allergy," Desiree joked, "I break out in handcuffs." 
 In October, she was back to regularly attending 12-step meetings, particularly one on Tuesdays for women only.

[TITLE]: A Disabled Man’s Death Triggers a Horrific Chain of Events | A&E
[METADATA/INTRO]: Son Deserved to ‘Pass in Peace’ Christensen’s failure to cooperate forced officers to break down the front door, thrusting them into her property, where they witnessed several red X’s painted across the windows and mirrors in the residence. Investigators then...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Son Deserved to ‘Pass in Peace’ 
 Christensen’s failure to cooperate forced officers to break down the front door, thrusting them into her property, where they witnessed several red X’s painted across the windows and mirrors in the residence. Investigators then discovered Lloyd’s decomposing remains on the bathroom floor. It was clear he had been dead for several days with no visible signs of trauma, according to police. 
 Officers found Christensen barricaded in her bedroom and tased her preceding her arrest. She was then transported to the police station for questioning. Christensen, a former certified nursing assistant and her son’s caregiver, agreed to speak with investigators without a lawyer present.
